title: Writing - Part 1: Sleep and Health
description: Your class has been discussing the importance of sleep for health and wellbeing, and why many people fail to get enough rest.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the causes of poor sleep in your notes. You should explain which cause you consider most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Using phones and other devices late at night disrupts the body's natural sleep cycle.
Work pressure and anxiety make it difficult for many adults to switch off at the end of the day.
Modern lifestyles, with shift work and irregular schedules, prevent people from establishing healthy sleep habits.

structure.intro: Introduce the importance of sleep and state the two causes you will discuss.
structure.body1: Discuss the first cause and its impact on sleep quality.
structure.body2: Discuss the second cause and compare its significance to the first.
structure.conclusion: State which cause you regard as most significant and suggest what could be done.

content.modelAnswer:
Sleep is essential for physical and mental health, yet millions of people worldwide fail to get the recommended amount. Among the various factors contributing to poor sleep, two stand out as particularly significant: excessive screen time and work-related stress.

The blue light emitted by smartphones, tablets and computers interferes with the production of melatonin, the hormone responsible for inducing sleep. When people use devices late at night, they make it harder for their bodies to recognise that it is time to rest. The engaging nature of social media and streaming services also makes it difficult to stop scrolling, further delaying sleep onset.

Work-related stress is another major cause. When people feel overwhelmed by professional demands, their minds remain active even after they have gone to bed. Lying awake worrying about deadlines, conflicts or financial concerns prevents the mental relaxation necessary for quality sleep.

In my view, work-related stress is the more serious cause. While screen time habits can be changed relatively easily by establishing a bedtime routine, stress is often tied to working conditions that individuals cannot easily control. Addressing workplace culture, reducing excessive workloads and encouraging employees to set boundaries are essential steps towards helping people get the rest they need.
